summary:
The Congenital Heart Futures Reauthorization Act of 2024 (Public Law 118-107) amends Section 399V-2 of the Public Health Service Act to extend and expand the federal government's national program for congenital heart disease research, surveillance, and awareness.

The bill's primary purpose is to reauthorize this program and to require new reporting and strategic planning related to congenital heart disease.

The Act makes three main changes. First, it adds a new requirement for the Secretary of Health and Human Services to issue a report to specified congressional committees (the House Committee on Energy and Commerce and the Senate Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions) within two years of enactment. This report must describe past and present federal activities to raise public awareness of congenital heart disease, assess efforts to educate and train health care providers, and describe the current U.S. workforce capacity of providers treating adult patients with the disease.

Second, the bill requires the Secretary, within one year after submitting that report, to develop and submit to Congress a strategy for improving public awareness and provider education and training. The strategy must include findings and recommendations to address gaps in public awareness and research regarding patients' lifelong needs (including long-term outcomes, quality of life, mental health, and health care utilization), address shortages in the workforce treating adult patients (potentially through fellowship or continuing education programs), and foster collaboration across federal agencies, providers, researchers, and patient organizations. In developing the strategy, the Secretary is to consult, as appropriate, with stakeholder groups such as patient organizations, health care professionals, research entities, insurers, accrediting organizations, and federal agencies including the CDC, NIH, and HRSA.

Third, the bill extends the program's authorization of appropriations, replacing the prior period of fiscal years 2020 through 2024 with fiscal years 2025 through 2029. It also redesignates existing subsections (f) and (g) as (g) and (h).

The Act was approved on November 21, 2024.
